marketNote: Slovenian household energy is priced per-unit (EUR/kWh energy) plus a small fixed monthly fee, then network charges + OVE/SPTE levy + 22% VAT on top; the government cap on household electricity ended 1 March 2025 and gas caps earlier, so as of 2026 there is NO active price cap and prices are fully market-set (households cluster near ~0.11-0.13 EUR/kWh energy ex-VAT, ~0.20 EUR/kWh all-in). Published energy prices below are ex-VAT (brez DDV) as suppliers list them; fixed monthly fees are shown incl. VAT.
_sourceNote: Primary source: ZPS (Slovenian Consumers' Association) monthly market survey, prices checked 3 July 2026 (published 6 July 2026). Cross-checked against supplier price-list pages. Electricity: VT=visja/high tariff (weekdays 06-22), MT=nizja/low tariff (nights + weekends/holidays), ET=single-tariff meter. Actual monthly bill = energy price x kWh used + fixed fee + regulated network charges + levies + VAT; it is NOT a flat subscription.

providers.notes: Slovenia's largest electricity supplier. e-invoice cuts the fixed fee roughly in half (2.43 -> 1.21 EUR/mo).

plans.name: Redni cenik (standard, gospodinjski odjemalci)
keyFeatures: e-invoice ECO discount lowers fixed fee to 1.21 EUR/mo
bundleDiscounts: can pair with GEN-I gas supply (dual-fuel from one supplier)
plans.notes: Energy price ex-VAT; fixed fee incl. VAT. Actual monthly cost depends on usage + network charges + levies + 22% VAT.

plans.name: GEN-I Fiksni (fixed-price)
plans.greenOrFixed: fixed price (guaranteed, valid from 23.10.2025 do preklica)
keyFeatures: price locked/guaranteed but no formal lock-in and no early-exit penalty
higher unit price than standard in exchange for price certainty
plans.notes: Also offers 'GEN-I Aktivni' = a different (multi-block/time-of-use) tariff system where the 8-month low season is ~3.5x cheaper 7h/day than peak; not a simple VT/MT plan. Actual monthly depends on usage + network + levies + VAT.

plans.name: Redna ponudba (gas)
plans.notes: Gas billed per kWh (Slovenia meters m3 then converts to kWh via caloric value). ZPS July-2026 gas survey lists no monthly fixed fee for GEN-I gas (only 'ni vezave; ni pogodbene kazni'), unlike ECE (2.03) and Energija plus (2.43) which ARE itemized - so a separate fixed fee, if any, is not published; left null. Plus network charges + VAT.

providers.notes: Fuel/retail group; ties energy to the Petrol klub loyalty program.

plans.name: Redni cenik (standard)
keyFeatures: Petrol klub perk: redeem 300 Zlate tocke to cut electricity cost by an extra 1 EUR/mo for the next 3 months (always available)
plans.notes: Energy ex-VAT, fixed fee incl. VAT. Actual monthly depends on usage + network + levies + VAT.
plans.source: https://www.petrol.si/ (cenik gospodinjski odjem EE) ; ZPS survey 3.7.2026

plans.name: Akcijski cenik (promo, fixed 1yr)
keyFeatures: same Petrol klub 300-tocke = -1 EUR/mo x3 perk
plans.notes: Fixed fee 1.98 EUR ex-VAT = 2.42 EUR incl. VAT. Actual monthly depends on usage + network + levies + VAT.

plans.name: Akcija Ugodno 2026 (gas, promo)
keyFeatures: free exit unless contract says otherwise
plans.notes: Standard (non-promo) Petrol gas = 0.05057 EUR/kWh. ZPS July-2026 gas survey does not itemize a monthly fixed fee for Petrol gas (unlike ECE 2.03 / Energija plus 2.43), so left null. Plus network + VAT.

providers.notes: Retail arm of the Elektro Maribor group; runs an explicit electricity+gas dual-fuel bundle ('Dvojni plus').

plans.name: Osnovna oskrba (basic supply)
keyFeatures: e-invoice discount lowers fixed fee to 1.22 EUR/mo
bundleDiscounts: 'Dvojni plus' bundles electricity + gas from one supplier
plans.notes: A green variant 'Osnovna oskrba OVE' adds a 0.003 EUR/kWh renewable-energy surcharge (VT 0.12779). Energy ex-VAT, fee incl. VAT.

plans.name: Akcija GOS 2025/3 (promo, fixed 12mo)
keyFeatures: early-exit compensation charged per metering point per remaining month
plans.notes: Valid from 1.10.2025 until revoked. Plus network + levies + VAT.

plans.name: Akcija Plin 2026 GOS (gas, promo)
keyFeatures: e-invoice discount 1.22 EUR/mo on fixed fee
plans.notes: Standard Energija plus gas (Osnovna / 'Plin' / Dvojni plus) = 0.05399 EUR/kWh with same 2.43 EUR fee. Plus network + VAT.

providers.notes: Supply company; 'Zanesljiva oskrba' (reliable supply) is its household flagship.

plans.name: Zanesljiva oskrba (standard)
keyFeatures: no fixed lock-in (early-exit penalty only if the contract stipulates)
e-invoice discount 0.99 EUR/mo on fixed fee
bundleDiscounts: can add Elektro energija gas ('Zanesljivo plin')
plans.notes: Energy ex-VAT, fee incl. VAT. Plus network + levies + VAT.

plans.name: Zanesljiva oskrba - fiksni (fixed)
keyFeatures: price locked 1 year from signing the annex
plans.notes: Fixed-price option trades a higher unit rate for price certainty. Plus network + levies + VAT.

plans.name: Zanesljivo plin (gas)
keyFeatures: no lock-in (early-exit penalty only if contract says)
plans.notes: One of the lower fixed monthly fees among gas offers. Plus network + VAT.

providers.notes: Ljubljana municipal utility; major gas supplier (sourced via national wholesaler Geoplin). Distinctive: NO fixed monthly fee on its electricity plans.

plans.name: Redna ponudba (standard electricity)
keyFeatures: NO monthly fixed fee (unusual in the market)
no lock-in (early-exit only recovers damages/benefits granted)
plans.notes: Zero fixed fee makes this attractive for low-usage households. Plus network + levies + VAT.

plans.name: Akcija 2026/1 (promo electricity)
keyFeatures: early-exit penalty (waived if switching to another offer from the same supplier)

plans.name: Akcija Ugodni plin 2026/1 (gas, promo)
keyFeatures: one of the cheapest promo gas unit prices in the survey
no monthly fixed fee (Energetika Ljubljana charges 'ni mesecnega stroska' across its offers)
early-exit recovers damages + a 5% non-offtake charge
plans.notes: Standard Energetika Ljubljana gas = 0.04995 EUR/kWh. Gas is sourced via Geoplin (national wholesaler). ZPS July-2026 gas survey lists no pavsalni strosek for Energetika Ljubljana, consistent with its zero-fixed-fee model on electricity ('ni mesecnega stroska') - fixed fee set to 0. Plus network + VAT.

providers.notes: Celje-area supplier (part of the Elektro Celje / E3 group); competitive fixed-price promos.

plans.name: ECE elektrika (standard)
keyFeatures: e-invoice discount lowers fixed fee to 1.22 EUR/mo
plans.notes: Energy ex-VAT, fee incl. VAT. Plus network + levies + VAT.

plans.name: Akcija ECE FIKS B25/12 (promo, fixed 1yr)
keyFeatures: early-exit compensation per metering point per remaining month

plans.name: Akcija Zanesljivi plin 26/1 (gas, promo)
keyFeatures: e-invoice discount 0.82 EUR/mo on the 2.03 EUR fixed fee
plans.notes: Standard ECE gas = 0.05398 EUR/kWh, fixed fee 2.03 EUR/mo (1.22 with e-invoice). Commitment length not stated - uncertain. Plus network + VAT.

regulatedComponents.note: On top of the supplier energy price, every SI household bill adds regulated network charges + levies + VAT. These are supplier-independent (set by the regulator / SODO-e-distribucija, not the energy seller), so they are the same whichever supplier the advisor recommends.
regulatedComponents._sourceNote: Figures are the Agencija za energijo / SURS reference for an AVERAGE Slovenian household, Q1 2026 (retail reference price 208.4 EUR/MWh, ~0.208 EUR/kWh all-in). Structure of that all-in price: energy delivery 56.4%, network charge (omreznina) 21.9%, levies 3.0%, excise 0.7%, VAT 18.0%. Sources: energetika-portal.si 'Cene elektricne energije v prvem cetrtletju 2026'; stat.si (SURS) Cene energentov; agen-rs.si tarifne postavke omreznine.
regulatedComponents.networkChargeNote: omreznina = the regulated charge for using the distribution + transmission grid. Since 1 Oct 2024 it is billed mostly on booked connection power (obracunska moc) across 5 seasonal time blocks, NOT purely per kWh, so a very-low-usage apartment pays proportionally MORE per kWh than the average below. For an average household it works out to ~45.6 EUR/MWh ex-VAT (~0.046 EUR/kWh), ~11% higher than a year earlier. Typical absolute figure: roughly 10-18 EUR/month for a small apartment regardless of usage, because most of it is the fixed power component.
regulatedComponents.oveSpteLevyNote: This 0.0061 EUR/kWh (6.1 EUR/MWh ex-VAT, Q1 2026) is the SURS 'levies' bundle = OVE+SPTE contribution (renewables + high-efficiency CHP support) PLUS the energy-efficiency contribution PLUS the market-operator fee, which SURS groups together. OVE+SPTE is the largest part. Note: households paid a temporary 50% OVE+SPTE reduction Nov 2025-Feb 2026; from 1 Mar 2026 the full rate is back, and under the new ZSROVE-1 law it is charged on actual consumed kWh rather than booked power.
regulatedComponents.trosarinaNote: Excise duty (trosarina) on electricity, 1.53 EUR/MWh ex-VAT (Q1 2026). Small relative to energy + network.
regulatedComponents.allInNote: Average all-in household electricity price ~0.208 EUR/kWh (208 EUR/MWh, Q1 2026, incl. energy + omreznina + levies + excise + 22% VAT). Rule of thumb the advisor can use: all-in ~ energy_price_incl_VAT + ~0.08-0.09 EUR/kWh of regulated network/levies/excise (also VAT-loaded). The supplier's energy price is only ~56% of the final bill, so switching supplier moves ~half the bill, not all of it.
regulatedComponents.gasRegulatedNote: For gas the same logic applies: on top of the ~0.045-0.054 EUR/kWh energy price, the bill adds distribution omreznina (gas network), CO2 dajatev (carbon levy on natural gas), trosarina na zemeljski plin (gas excise), prispevek za energetsko ucinkovitost, and 22% VAT. All-in household gas typically lands around ~0.08-0.09 EUR/kWh incl. everything. Gas regulated charges are set by the regulator + distribution operator, not the supplier.

sampleMonthlyBill.assumption: 250 kWh/month apartment (low-usage, single household), average-supplier + average regulated charges, using the SURS Q1-2026 all-in price and cost structure.
sampleMonthlyBill.splitNote: 250 kWh x ~0.208 EUR/kWh all-in = ~52 EUR/month. Breakdown uses the SURS structure (energy 56.4% incl. the ~2.43 EUR supplier fixed fee, network 21.9%, levies 3.0%, excise 0.7%, VAT 18.0%). Reality check: because omreznina is now mostly a fixed power charge, a genuinely tiny apartment can see network land higher (~13-16 EUR) and the energy share lower. The advisor should treat this as an order-of-magnitude estimate, not a quote. Only the ~29 EUR energy slice moves when you switch supplier.
